ogTitle: Dirty Creek vs Doctor George Mountain: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dirty Creek and Doctor George Mountain.
heading: Dirty Creek vs Doctor George Mountain — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dirty Creek, New South Wales and Doctor George Mountain, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dirty Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Doctor George Mountain (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dirty Creek or Doctor George Mountain safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dirty Creek has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Doctor George Mountain scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dirty Creek scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Doctor George Mountain scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dirty Creek or Doctor George Mountain?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dirty Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
